task.title: Sanitation checkpoints and git history policy
task.summary: Keep hot wiki state small by relying on git for full history and storing only compact semantic archives and release checkpoints.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki distinguishes hot current truth, generated views, compact semantic history, and full git recovery.
acceptance: Closed task archive stores compact semantic summaries with commit/release links rather than full raw context by default.
Release checkpoint schema records version, git sha, canonical digest, view schema version, and closed task summaries.
Docs and skills describe commit/push/version boundaries.
Views exclude cold history unless explicitly requested.
non_goals: Automatically push or version without policy gates.

delta.desired: Sanitation keeps agent hot context small while preserving semantic memory and git recovery.
delta.current: Retention exists but archives and release/version boundaries are not fully aligned with git as full history.
delta.closure: Archive/checkpoint policy is implemented and documented.
